# Clinekt Health

> Clinekt Health is the AI agent platform for patient activation: turning inactive patients into booked appointments. A team of three AI agents (Inbound, Recall, Outbound) engages every website visitor, brings dormant patients back from the EHR, and generates net-new demand, screening and booking qualified patients 24/7 in the practice's own brand.

Patient activation covers the three places practices lose patients: website visitors who leave without booking, existing patients who go dormant in the EHR, and net-new demand that never finds the practice. Clinekt is not a chatbot, not a contact form, not a voice answering service, and not reactivation software. The Inbound Agent runs specialty-trained screening and books website visitors; the Recall Agent works dormant EHR charts back into open slots; the Outbound Agent generates new demand and traces every patient from first click to completed care. The agents work alongside Epic, athenahealth, ModMed, NextGen, eClinicalWorks, and other major EHRs with no IT project, and go live in under a week. HIPAA compliant, SOC 2 Type II, AES-256 encryption.

Platform results: over 1 million patient interactions completed, 24x average ROI, +8% appointment volume, 48% reduction in unqualified consults. Example: Baldwin Bone and Joint, a multi-provider orthopedic group, generated 263 qualified surgical leads with a 60% booking rate in a single quarter.

## Core Pages

- [Home](https://clinekthealth.com/): AI agents that activate every patient
- [What Is Patient Activation?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/what-is-patient-activation): Category definition, the three-agent model, and how patient activation differs from patient engagement and reactivation software
- [Voice AI vs. Reactivation Software vs. Patient Activation Agents](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/voice-ai-vs-reactivation-software-vs-patient-activation-agents): Plain-English comparison of the AI patient tool categories
- [Case Studies](https://clinekthealth.com/case-studies): Results from specialty practices using Clinekt
- [Baldwin Bone and Joint Case Study](https://clinekthealth.com/case-studies/baldwin-bone-joint-ai-case-study): 263 qualified surgical leads and a 60% booking rate in one quarter
- [What Is Patient Leakage?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/what-is-patient-leakage): Where practices lose patients they already earned, and how to measure it
- [How to Reduce Patient Leakage](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/how-to-reduce-patient-leakage): Seven strategies that close the website, EHR, and referral leaks, with the statistics behind them
- [Patient Leakage Calculator](https://clinekthealth.com/leakage-calculator): Run your own leakage numbers in under two minutes
- [Ten Fair Questions, Answered](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/what-do-practices-ask-before-adopting-patient-activation): The questions practices ask before adopting patient activation, answered straight
- [FAQs](https://clinekthealth.com/faqs): How the patient activation agents work, including what happens to existing forms, schedulers, and answering services
- [Book a Demo](https://clinekthealth.com/book-a-demo): 15-minute demo, white-labeled to your practice
- [Blog](https://clinekthealth.com/blog): Patient activation insights for specialty practices

## Guides and Statistics (published 2026-07-16)

- [What's the Difference Between Patient Engagement and Patient Activation?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/patient-engagement-vs-patient-activation): Patient engagement is what your organization does. Patient activation is what the patient does next. See why that difference predicts outcomes and revenue.
- [What Does an AI Front Desk Actually Do in a Specialty Practice?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/what-does-an-ai-front-desk-do): What an AI front desk for medical practices actually does: the calls it resolves, the tasks it should never touch, and how it changes staffing.
- [What is the average patient no-show rate, and what does each missed appointment cost?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/average-no-show-rate-and-cost): Every no-show benchmark with its study, year, and setting: 23% worldwide, 6.81% US single-specialty, $196 per missed visit, and the $150B lineage.
- [What Should an AI Front Desk Handle in an Orthopedic Practice?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/ai-front-desk-for-orthopedic-practices): The orthopedic call taxonomy, subspecialty routing rules, and referral math behind an AI front desk that works, drawn from live ortho deployments.
- [How Do Patients Want to Book Medical Appointments? The Statistics Practices Should Know](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/patient-scheduling-statistics): Patient self-scheduling statistics for 2026: what patients say, how they actually book, and the provider adoption gap. Every stat sourced, dated, and citable.
- [How Do You Reactivate Dormant Patients Without Hiring More Staff?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/how-to-reactivate-dormant-patients): The operational playbook for reactivating dormant patients: EHR list pulls, warm/cool/cold segmentation, and multi-channel outreach that books visits.
- [How Much Revenue Does Patient Leakage Actually Cost a Specialty Practice?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/how-much-does-patient-leakage-cost): No verified universal number exists for patient leakage cost. See which statistics are real, which are folklore, and calculate your own number.
- [How many patients try to book appointments after hours, and where do they go instead?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/after-hours-patient-booking): After hours appointment booking statistics: 43% of bookings happen outside office hours, 14% overnight. See where that demand goes and how to capture it.
- [Are AI Receptionists and Patient Outreach Agents HIPAA Compliant?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/are-ai-receptionists-hipaa-compliant): A plain-English HIPAA guide for vetting AI receptionists: where PHI actually lives, the BAA clauses that matter, and a 12-question vendor checklist.
- [Appointment Reminders vs Patient Recall vs Patient Reactivation: What's the Difference?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/reminders-vs-recall-vs-reactivation): Reminders confirm booked visits, recall books patients due for care, reactivation wins back dormant ones. The full taxonomy with a decision tree.
- [How does AI patient scheduling actually work, and what does EHR integration really mean?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/how-ai-patient-scheduling-works): AI patient scheduling explained: voice agents, self-scheduling, recall outreach, and the EHR write-back question every vendor dodges.
- [Which Patient Engagement Software Does a Specialty Practice Actually Need?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/patient-engagement-software-for-specialty-practices): A specialty-segmented buyer's guide to patient engagement software: six tool categories, per-specialty fit, hidden fees, and what to measure in a pilot.
- [How Much Does an AI Receptionist Cost for a Medical Practice?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/how-much-does-an-ai-receptionist-cost): The three AI receptionist pricing models, the hidden fees vendors leave off the page, and a per-specialty ROI worksheet for your practice.
- [Do Appointment Reminder Texts Actually Reduce No-Shows?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/do-appointment-reminders-reduce-no-shows): Do appointment reminder texts reduce no-shows? Yes: controlled studies show 28.9-39.1% fewer missed visits. See the evidence and why reminders alone plateau.
- [What percentage of referrals never get scheduled, and how do you stop losing yours?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/what-percent-of-referrals-never-get-scheduled): Studies report referral completion anywhere from 34.8% to 79.2%. We reconcile the numbers and give specialty practices a referral capture playbook.
- [How Do You Measure Patient Activation, and What If You Can't License the PAM?](https://clinekthealth.com/blog/how-to-measure-patient-activation): The PAM explained in plain English: 13 items, four levels, licensing reality, and the behavioral proxy metrics practices can track instead.
